cases.description: On the first turn of a fresh session, the agent greets with project state + workflow menu before doing anything else.
cases.prompt: (start of new session, no preceding user input — synthetic first turn)
expectations: Agent's first output names the active project (or 'none')
Agent's first output names the in-flight workflow (or 'none active')
Agent's first output enumerates all six available workflows: project, feature, bug-fix, refactor, migration, quality-gates
Agent's first output references the output mode (caveman | normal)

cases.description: When asked which auth mode to use without context, agent recommends one with reasoning instead of asking the user to choose blank.
expectations: Agent does NOT respond with 'Do you want service-account or oauth_user?' (a blank choice).
Agent's response contains the phrase 'Recommend' or its equivalent before any choice prompt.
Agent's recommendation is justified with at least one specific reason tied to the user's environment (active gcloud account, prior project config, etc).

cases.description: When the user describes a task without naming a workflow, agent picks the matching one and asks for confirmation.
cases.prompt: There's a bug — add(0, 0) returns 1 instead of 0.
expectations: Agent proposes the bug-fix workflow.
Agent's proposal explains the choice (e.g., 'this matches the bug-fix workflow because the user is reporting a failing scenario').
Agent ends with a 'Run bug-fix? (y/redirect)' style confirmation, not a silent invocation.

cases.description: On a phase transition gate, 'looks good' should NOT pass; the agent re-prompts for explicit approval.
expectations: Agent does NOT execute the phase transition.
Agent re-prompts: explicit 'ok' (case-insensitive, exactly two chars) or 'redirect <reason>' is required.
Agent restates the gate context briefly so the user knows what they're approving.

cases.description: Typos near the gate word ('appproved', 'okay', 'okk', 'okkk') should NOT pass — only the literal two-character ok (case-insensitive) qualifies.
expectations: Agent does NOT execute the phase transition (typo or near-miss is treated as ambiguous, not approval).
Agent re-prompts naming the literal two-character requirement.
Agent does not silently 'autocorrect' the typo into approval.

cases.description: 'okay' (4 chars) should NOT pass — the gate requires exactly two characters.
expectations: Agent does NOT execute the phase transition.
Agent re-prompts noting that 'okay' is one char too long.

cases.description: 'OK' (uppercase) DOES pass — the match is case-insensitive on the literal two characters.
expectations: Agent executes the phase transition (calls 'devloop transition --approve').
Agent confirms the transition succeeded.
No re-prompt — the literal two-character match is satisfied regardless of case.
